query: Johor state election 2026 expected outcome, green wave PN Perikatan Nasional Malay voters, Barisan Nasional incumbency, Onn Hafiz popularity
answer:
I've completed the research on the Johor state election 2026. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**Election basics:** Polling day is **11 July 2026** (advance voting 7 July), following Onn Hafiz Ghazi's early dissolution of the assembly on 1 June 2026 — a gambit roughly a year ahead of schedule. All 56 seats are contested with 172 candidates.

**Expected outcome:** Analysts broadly assess **"Advantage Barisan Nasional."** BN retaining control is described as "almost a foregone conclusion" — the open question is the seat margin (BN holds 40/56 currently; 40 is its cited benchmark). The Edge Malaysia's published projections rate a pro-BN sweep (BN 53, PH 3, PN 0) as most likely (>60%).

**Green wave / PN status:** The PN/PAS "green wave" is assessed as **weakened and divided** in Johor — PN contests only 33 of 56 seats, PAS-Bersatu ground cooperation is poor (fragmenting the Malay vote to BN's benefit), PAS is historically weak in Johor, and Bersatu lost key figure Muhyiddin Yassin. A pro-PN outcome is rated <10% likely. PN nonetheless retains strength in conservative religious sentiment and cost-of-living grievances.

**BN incumbency & Onn Hafiz popularity:** Underpinned by Onn Hafiz's youthful/technocratic appeal and UMNO-founder lineage, Johor's strong economy (6.4% growth in 2025, record investment), robust UMNO machinery, and a remarkably consistent ~600,000-vote base.

**Notable dynamic:** BN and PH — both federal Madani government partners — are contesting each other in all 56 seats (14 straight fights), the first such clash at scale in Peninsular Malaysia.

The full report and structured data (key facts, sources, and 17 quantitative data points) have been recorded via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: 2026 Johor state election
sources.snippet: Election expected 11 July 2026; dissolution announced 1 June 2026 by Onn Hafiz Ghazi; all 56 seats contested.

sources.title: The 2026 Johor Election: Advantage Barisan Nasional?
sources.snippet: BN's consistent ~600,000 votes, PN implosion, Onn Hafiz popularity, 40-seat benchmark; state-to-parliament weak read-across (40/56 state vs 9/26 parliament).

sources.title: Opinion: Projecting the Johor state election results
sources.snippet: Three seat-projection scenarios: pro-BN (BN 53, PH 3, PN 0, >60%), pro-PH (BN 39, PH 14, PN 3), pro-PN (PN 25, BN 21, PH 10, <10%).

sources.title: Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections are double trouble for Anwar
sources.snippet: BN incumbent running solo around Onn Hafiz; PN (PAS-dominated) openly divided but a force; PH partners clash; RM110bn record investment 2025.

sources.title: Johor Polls: BN, PH Lead Field With 56 Candidates Each
sources.snippet: BN 56 seats, PH 56, PN 33, Bersama 15, MUDA 4, PSM 1, ASLI 1 (debut).

sources.title: BN and PH go head-to-head in 14 Johor seats
sources.snippet: 14 straight fights (all BN vs PH), 27 three-cornered, 12 four-cornered, 3 five-cornered; polling 11 July, early voting 7 July.

sources.title: EC: Johor election features 172 candidates, BN and PH contest all 56 seats
sources.snippet: EC confirms 172 candidates (138 male, 34 female); no disqualifications.

sources.title: Malaysia's Johor election campaign begins as federal allies clash
sources.snippet: PH and BN both contest all 56 seats alongside 60 other candidates in a crowded 14-day race.

sources.title: BN-PH contests in Johor polls reflect democratic maturity, say analysts
sources.snippet: Analysts say BN-PH clashes in all 56 seats do not threaten federal unity government stability.

key_facts: Polling day is 11 July 2026 (advance voting 7 July); the Johor assembly was dissolved 1 June 2026 by MB Onn Hafiz Ghazi, calling polls ~1 year early.
All 56 seats of the 16th Johor State Legislative Assembly are contested; BN currently holds 40/56 (won outright majority in 2022).
172 candidates total: BN 56 seats, PH 56, PN 33, Bersama 15, MUDA 4, PSM 1, ASLI 1, plus 6 independents.
Contest breakdown: 14 straight fights (all BN vs PH), 27 three-cornered, 12 four-cornered, 3 five-cornered.
BN and PH — both federal Madani government partners — are contesting each other in all 56 seats, a first for PH partners at this scale in Peninsular Malaysia.
BN runs solo, having rejected cooperation with DAP; Onn Hafiz framed it as ideological, not racial.
Analysts (Fulcrum/ISEAS, East Asia Forum) assess 'Advantage Barisan Nasional'; BN retaining control is called 'almost a foregone conclusion.'
Onn Hafiz is popular: youthful, technocratic, direct lineage to UMNO founders; increased social assistance and affordable housing spending.
Johor grew 6.4% in 2025 (fastest state) and drew record approved investment (RM426.7 billion in 2025; ~RM110bn FDI cited).
BN has polled a consistent ~600,000 votes across 2018, 2022 state, and 2022 GE15 elections.
The 'green wave' (PN/PAS) is weakened and divided in Johor: poor PAS-Bersatu cooperation fragments the Malay vote; PAS historically weak in Johor; Bersatu lost Muhyiddin Yassin.
PN is contesting only 33 of 56 seats, signaling reduced reach vs 2022.
The Edge Malaysia projects pro-BN scenario (>60% likelihood: BN 53, PH 3, PN 0) as most likely; pro-PN scenario rated <10%.
40 seats is BN's cited benchmark; below that would read as a strategic failure given the early-election gambit.
Weak state-to-parliament read-across: BN won 40/56 state seats in 2022 but only 9/26 Johor parliamentary seats in GE15.

data_points.metric: Total seats in Johor State Legislative Assembly contested
BN consistent vote base across last 3 elections
Projected BN seats - pro-BN scenario (>60% likelihood)
Projected PN seats - pro-PN scenario (<10% likelihood)
BN Johor parliamentary seats won in GE15 2022 (out of 26)
